reforms.description: Korea launched a three-year plan to promote the development of a knowledge-based economy, focusing on improving the information infrastructure and strengthening the R&D framework. The government aims to increase the role of universities in the R&D effort by boosting incentives for research by professors, thereby enhancing the overall research capacity in the country.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the R&D framework and university involvement is expected to improve research capacity and innovation output.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been successfully implemented as part of the three-year plan.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a structural change in the R&D framework and university involvement in research.
reforms.source_quote: Increase the role of universities in the R&D effort by boosting incentives for research by professors.

reforms.description: The government established Dasan Venture in 2001 to provide seed money and management know-how to promising startups, enhancing the venture capital landscape.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing seed funding and management support to startups is expected to stimulate innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative has been successfully established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative significantly enhances the support available for startups, contributing to the overall ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Most important is Dasan Venture, established in 2001 to provide seed money and management know-how to promising start-ups.

reforms.description: SMEs may choose a tax credit equal to 15 percent of R&D expenditures in each business year, incentivizing research and development activities.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The tax credit is designed to encourage SMEs to invest in R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The tax credit has been successfully implemented and is available to SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a tax credit for R&D expenditures represents a significant incentive for SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: SMEs may choose instead a tax credit equal to 15 per cent of R&D expenditures in each business year.

reforms.description: The government should adopt fundamental reforms in the R&D system and the venture business policy framework to encourage the growth of new industries, addressing deficiencies at the university level and enhancing interaction between universities, government research institutes, and the private sector. Plans include promoting labor mobility and enhancing opportunities for networking to facilitate knowledge transfer.
reforms.package_name: R&D System and Venture Policy Reform
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reforming the R&D system and enhancing collaboration between sectors is expected to drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been proposed and are awaiting implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed reforms represent a significant overhaul of the R&D system and venture policies.
reforms.source_quote: Encourage interaction between R&D in universities, the private sector and government research institutes by promoting labor mobility and enhancing opportunities for networking.

reforms.description: The 2002 budget included a significant increase in R&D investment in science and technology, reflecting the government's commitment to enhance the country's growth potential through innovation. Additionally, the government established the National Science and Technology Council to coordinate R&D priorities across various ministries, enhancing the efficiency of public R&D funding.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D investment and improving coordination are expected to enhance the effectiveness of public funding and drive innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The budget increase and the establishment of the council have been successfully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The significant increase in R&D funding and the establishment of a coordinating body represent major reforms in public R&D policy.
reforms.source_quote: The Science and Technology Framework Law of 2001 established a co-ordinating body – the National Science and Technology Council – to set priorities in this area.

reforms.description: The Brain-Korea 21 programme was initiated to strengthen the role of universities in research by providing significant funding to selected institutions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Providing funding to universities is expected to enhance their research capabilities and contribute to innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been successfully initiated and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant investment in university research capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The government is trying to strengthen the universities’ role in research through the Brain-Korea 21 programme, which is spending 1.4 trillion won on selected universities during the 1999 to 2005 period.

reforms.description: The 21st Century Frontier Programme was launched to fund research projects in key areas expected to drive industrial innovation over the next decade.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Funding targeted research projects in key areas is expected to stimulate industrial innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme has been proposed and is awaiting implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant investment in targeted sectoral R&D.
reforms.source_quote: Another aspect of the R&D effort was the 1999 launch of the 21st Century Frontier Programme, a ten-year project that is to spend 2 trillion won on about twenty research projects that are expected to be a new source of industrial innovation.

reforms.description: The government reduced mandatory contributions from service providers for R&D from 1% of revenue to between 0.5% and 0.75%, aiming to lower the financial burden on telecommunications companies and encourage investment in innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing the financial burden on telecommunications companies is expected to encourage more investment in R&D and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The reduction in contributions has been successfully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reduction in mandatory contributions represents a significant adjustment to the financial obligations of service providers.
reforms.source_quote: The mandatory contributions from service providers for R&D was reduced from 1 per cent of revenue in 2001 to between 0.5 and 0.75 per cent in June 2002.
